content: The semantic similarity scores output by Sentence Transformers can be re-scaled through a domain-adaptive sigmoid activation to calibrate similarity distributions to human perception of semantic relatedness. Unlike the raw unbounded cosine similarity scores that Sentence Transformers typically output, a tuned sigmoid compresses similarity into a 0-1 range that naturally aligns with how humans rate semantic relatedness (completely unrelated = 0, identical meaning = 1), while also suppressing noisy outlier similarity scores that arise from off-topic token overlap between unrelated sentences.
